quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 4 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: Force-Time Graphs — Impulse, Momentum, and Velocity
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question:
The magnitude of a force applied to a $3.0\text{ kg}$ block initially at rest along a horizontal frictionless surface is shown in the graph below.

Calculate the total impulse delivered to the block between $t = 0\text{ s}$ and $t = 15\text{ s}$.
questions.explanation: Area = Triangle (0–5 s) + Rectangle (5–10 s) + Triangle (10–12 s). $\frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 15 + 5 \times 15 + \frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 15 = 37.5 + 75.0 + 15.0 = 127.5\text{ N s}$.

questions.question:
The magnitude of a force applied to a $3.0\text{ kg}$ block initially at rest along a horizontal frictionless surface is shown in the graph below.

Determine the velocity of the block at $t = 5\text{ s}$.
questions.explanation: Impulse from $t = 0$ to $5\text{ s}$ = area of triangle = $\frac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 15 = 37.5\text{ N s}$. $v_f = J / m = 37.5 / 3.0 = 12.5\text{ m/s}$.

questions.question:
The magnitude of a force applied to a $3.0\text{ kg}$ block initially at rest along a horizontal frictionless surface is shown in the graph below.

At what time is the block's momentum at its maximum value?
questions.explanation: Momentum increases as long as force is positive. The force is positive from $t = 0$ to $t = 12\text{ s}$, then drops to zero. Maximum momentum occurs at $t = 12\text{ s}$.

questions.question:
The magnitude of a force applied to a $3.0\text{ kg}$ block initially at rest along a horizontal frictionless surface is shown in the graph below.

What is the value of the maximum momentum of the block?
questions.explanation: Maximum momentum = total impulse up to $t = 12\text{ s}$. Area = Triangle (0–5 s) + Rectangle (5–10 s) + Triangle (10–12 s) = $37.5 + 75.0 + 15.0 = 127.5\text{ kg m/s}$.

questions.question:
The magnitude of a force applied to a $3.0\text{ kg}$ block initially at rest along a horizontal frictionless surface is shown in the graph below.

Calculate the magnitude of the slope of the force-time graph between $t = 10\text{ s}$ and $t = 12\text{ s}$.
questions.explanation: Slope $= \Delta F / \Delta t = (0 - 15) / (12 - 10) = -15 / 2 = -7.5\text{ N/s}$. Magnitude $= 7.5\text{ N/s}$.

questions.question:
A net force $F(t)$ acts along the x-axis on a $2.5\text{ kg}$ object initially moving at $10.0\text{ m/s}$ in the positive x-direction. The force-time relationship is shown in the graph below.

Determine the impulse delivered to the object during the interval $t = 0\text{ s}$ to $t = 8\text{ s}$.
questions.explanation: Area from $t = 0$ to $6\text{ s}$: triangle = $\frac{1}{2} \times 6 \times 20 = 60\text{ N s}$. From $t = 6$ to $8\text{ s}$: trapezoid. $F(8) = 20 + (-20/6 \times 2) = 13.33\text{ N}$. Area = $\frac{20 + 13.33}{2} \times 2 = 33.33\text{ N s}$. Total = $93.3\text{ N s}$.

questions.question:
A net force $F(t)$ acts along the x-axis on a $2.5\text{ kg}$ object initially moving at $10.0\text{ m/s}$ in the positive x-direction. The force-time relationship is shown in the graph below.

Determine the impulse delivered to the object during the interval $t = 0\text{ s}$ to $t = 9\text{ s}$.
questions.explanation: Area from $t = 0$ to $6\text{ s}$: triangle = $\frac{1}{2} \times 6 \times 20 = 60\text{ N s}$. From $t = 6$ to $9\text{ s}$: trapezoid. $F(9) = 10\text{ N}$ (midpoint). Area = $\frac{20 + 10}{2} \times 3 = 45\text{ N s}$. Total = $105\text{ N s}$.

questions.question:
A net force $F(t)$ acts along the x-axis on a $2.5\text{ kg}$ object initially moving at $10.0\text{ m/s}$ in the positive x-direction. The force-time relationship is shown in the graph below.

Calculate the change in velocity of the object from $t = 0\text{ s}$ to $t = 12\text{ s}$.
questions.explanation: Total impulse = area of triangle = $\frac{1}{2} \times 12 \times 20 = 120\text{ N s}$. $\Delta v = J / m = 120 / 2.5 = 48\text{ m/s}$.

questions.question:
A net force $F(t)$ acts along the x-axis on a $2.5\text{ kg}$ object initially moving at $10.0\text{ m/s}$ in the positive x-direction. The force-time relationship is shown in the graph below.

What is the velocity of the object at $t = 16\text{ s}$?
questions.explanation: Total impulse up to $t = 12\text{ s}$ = $120\text{ N s}$. No additional impulse after $t = 12\text{ s}$. $J = m(v_f - v_i)$. $120 = 2.5(v_f - 10.0)$. $v_f = 48 + 10.0 = 58.0\text{ m/s}$.

questions.question:
A net force $F(t)$ acts along the x-axis on a $2.5\text{ kg}$ object initially moving at $10.0\text{ m/s}$ in the positive x-direction. The force-time relationship is shown in the graph below.

If the object's kinetic energy is $450\text{ J}$ at $t = 0\text{ s}$, what is its kinetic energy at $t = 16\text{ s}$?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered force-time graphs, impulse, and momentum calculations!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review finding impulse as area under F-t graphs and using $J = m\Delta v$.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on breaking graphs into geometric shapes and applying the impulse-momentum theorem.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: impulse = area under F-t graph, $J = \Delta p = m(v_f - v_i)$, and how slope relates to rate of change of force.
